issues.description: 4 of 5 unit tests fail with ValidationError at import time. The handler imports broker from app.py, which calls _create_app() at module level (line 100), which instantiates Settings() (line 34) requiring pg_dsn and embed_url. Unit tests cannot provide these without full infrastructure setup, violating test isolation.
issues.requirement: AC-009: Unit tests use TestNatsBroker with proper isolation
issues.suggestion: Refactor app.py to defer Settings() instantiation until the lifespan context manager runs, not at module-load time. Options: (1) Use lazy initialization pattern, (2) Accept settings as a parameter to _create_app(), (3) Move broker creation inside the lifespan. This will allow unit tests to import the handler module without triggering full app initialization.

issues.description: BDD oracle executed zero scenarios (scenarios_passed=0, one pytest_runner_error finding the feature file). This is ABSENT SIGNAL per absence-of-failure guard #1.
issues.requirement: BDD verification of relay integration behavior
issues.suggestion: Ensure the BDD feature file at features/relay-integration/relay-integration.feature is discoverable by pytest-bdd. Check the pytest collection path configuration and verify the feature file exists at the expected location.

issues.description: Wiring analysis shows parse_degraded status for app.py, handler.py, and settings.py. Cannot independently verify the @broker.subscriber registration path and import side-effect wiring.
issues.requirement: AC-001: Durable subscriber registered and wired via import side-effect
issues.suggestion: After fixing the Settings instantiation issue, verify the wiring analysis can successfully parse the files and confirm the registration path.

VERIFIED: test_settings_expose_dlq_configuration PASSED. Settings correctly exposes dlq_subject='MEMORY.DLQ', max_deliver=5, chunk_target_tokens=1000, chunk_overlap_ratio=0.15 with FLEET_MEMORY_ prefix.
Cannot verify: wiring parse_degraded (ABSENT SIGNAL), tests failed preventing code path verification
REJECTED: Tests fail at import time before TestNatsBroker setup can execute. This violates unit test isolation principles — handler cannot be imported without triggering full app initialization.
Likely verified: Player reports 'ruff check' passed. Linting is orthogonal to test failures, though cannot independently confirm without tool access.

rationale: Rejecting due to critical test failures (4 of 5 tests FAILED, 80% failure rate) caused by module-level Settings() instantiation in app.py. This violates test isolation — unit tests cannot import the handler without providing database and embed service configuration. AC-007 verified (Settings test passed), AC-010 likely verified (linting), but AC-001 through AC-006, AC-008, and AC-009 cannot be verified due to ABSENT SIGNAL from failed tests and parse_degraded wiring status. BDD oracle also shows zero scenarios executed (ABSENT SIGNAL). The architectural issue must be fixed to enable test execution and proper verification of all acceptance criteria.
